methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $250M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 11 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

private.name: True Anomaly

private.name: Hidden Level

data.narrative:
Kodiak Robotics, a Series C-stage defense and security robotics company that has raised $250M+, is evaluated against a universe of 11 peers in the same sector and stage band, comprising 2 public comparables and 8 private companies including True Anomaly, Saronic, and Chaos Industries.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $250M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yields $425M. Peer-median valuation across the 11-company defense and security universe produces $1.4B, anchored by True Anomaly and Vannevar Labs at $1.5B in Series C and B respectively, with the peer range spanning $500M to $9.2B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $361M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $912M. The wide dispersion between the stage-progression and peer methods reflects material uncertainty around growth trajectory and investor appetite within the defense sector. Key drivers of valuation within this range include Kodiak's position relative to higher-valued Series D peers like Saronic ($9.2B) and the relative comparability to True Anomaly ($1.5B) at an identical stage. The peer range width suggests significant heterogeneity in how capital markets value defense robotics companies, warranting focus on product differentiation, customer traction, and defensibility as primary value drivers.
